english: bai sta-gón-la (bai sta gon la) bźeṅs (bzhengs) he rose to make preparations for dinner Mil.
hip, hip-bone, e.g. as the seat of strength Mil.; stá-zúr yan-čád (sta zur yan chad) from the hip upward Dzl.

W., originally sta-grí (sta gri) Mil. and C., sta-ré (sta re) B., axe, hatchet; dgrá-sta (dgra sta) battle-axe Lex.; star-ltág (star ltag) Cs. the back of an axe or hatchet, star-míg (star mig) the hole for putting the handle in, star-yú (star yu) the handle, star-só (star so) the edge of an axe.

1. tiger, rgya-stág (rgya stag) the Bengal tiger Mil.; stag-prug (stag phrug) a young tiger, stág-mo (stag mo) a tigress; stag-tshaṅ (stag tshang) a tiger's den; stag-rís (stag ris) the stripes of a tiger's skin. - 2. Tar. 166, 2?
Mil. utensils carried by men about them, such as a knife, smoking-implements, weapons etc.
birch-tree; stág-ma (stag ma) n. of another tree.
a not unfrequent form (which prob. has been adapted to Tibetan etymology) for ta-zig (ta zig), Persia, Persian.
a medicinal herb, Glr., Med.; stag-śa-dé-ba (stag sha de ba) Glr.
a youth, young man C., Mil.
Cs.: n. of a black stone, acc. to Zam. a silver-ore.

Sch. also stáṅ-ka (stang ka), manner, style, posture, góm-pai staṅs (gom pai stangs) manner of walking, gait; brdég-staṅs byéd-pa (brdeg stangs byed pa) to assume a fighting posture Mil.; ltá-staṅs (lta stangs) v. ltá-ba (lta ba) comp.; stón-pai bźugs-staṅs (ston pai bzhugs stangs) the sitting posture of Buddha; C.: *ḳo gho-ghoṅ-taṅ dé-mo* (*ko gho ghong tang de mo*) his style of dressing is fine, he is well dressed; *tám-zer-taṅ ḱé-pa* (*tam zer tang khe pa*) eloquent; even like a mere termination for forming verbal substantives: *zá-taṅ* (*za tang*), or *túṅ-taṅ lég-mo* (*tung tang leg mo*) good eating, drinking.
pf. and fut. bstad (bstad), imp. stod (stod), to put on, to lay on, rtá-la sga (rta la sga) to put the saddle on a horse, to saddle; rtá-la gró-čas (rta la gro chas) to load the baggage on a horse.

mat, carpet, esp. a carpet for sitting on, also a cushion, resp. bźúgs-gdan (bzhugs gdan); saddle-cloth; stan ∘diṅ-ba (stan 'ding ba) to spread a mat (on the ground), ∘gébs-pa ('gebs pa) to lay (a mat) on; *ču-stán* (*chu stan*) swaddling-cloth W.; *bol-tén* (*bol ten*) mattress, *ṭul-tén* (*ṭul ten*) (lit. ṭrul-stán (ṭrul stan)) a light travelling-mattress C.; sometimes substratum of any kind, also of hard materials, e.g rtsub-stán (rtsub stan), btsab-stán (btsab stan).
1. v. rtab (rtab). - 2. Sch.: stab stáṅs-pa (stab stangs pa) to suffer, to tolerate, to yield.

(cog. to tabs (tabs), also syn. of staṅs (stangs)), mode, manner, way, measure, séṅ-gei stabs-kyis (seng gei stabs kyis) (or su (su)) gró-ba (gro ba) to walk in the manner of a lion; gar-stabs (gar stabs) v. gar (gar); opportunity, byóṅ-stabs (byong stabs) an opportunity for going; *tábs-śi ḱá-na* (*tabs shi kha na*) (also *ḱá-ne* (*kha ne*), or *ḱá-la* (*kha la*)) W when an opportunity offers; ríṅs-stabs-su (rings stabs su) hastily, speedily Mil.; *kón-stabs* (*kon stabs*) dearth, famine, want Ld.; *ríṅ-stabs* (*ring stabs*) a describing at full length, copiousness (stabs (stabs), in this instance, corresponds to the English termination 'ness', changing the adj. into an abstract noun).
for sta-rí (sta ri) q.v.

Sch., stár-ga (star ga) Lex., stár-Ka (star Ka) Glr., walnut, star-(gai) śiṅ (star gai shing), ljoṅ-śiṅ stár-ka (ljong shing star ka) walnut-tree Glr.; star-skógs (star skogs) nut-shell; star-sdóṅ (star sdong) trunk of a walnut-tree. stár-ka byéd-pa (star ka byed pa) Ld.-Glr. Schl. f 15, b (?).

pf. and fut. bstar (bstar), imp. stor (stor), 1. to file on a string, e.g. pearls; to tie fast, to fasten to, e.g. sheep to a rope, in a bivouac, stár-la rgyúd-pa (star la rgyud pa) id. - 2. to clean, to polish Lex. - 3. Sch.: to ornament, decorate (?).
or star-źúṅ (star zhung) Med., frq., the berries of Hippophaë rhamnoides, a shrub or tree very frequent in Tibet; acc. to a Lex. also a kind of Rumex in India.

pf. bstis (bstis), fut. bsti (bsti), imp. stis (stis), 1. to rest, to repose, to refresh one's self, sti-(bai) gnas (sti bai gnas) resting-place. - 2. to honour (?); (b)sti-stáṅ ((b)sti stang) honour, respect, reverence, byéd-pa (byed pa) ccd., to show a person honour, frq.; *ḱó-la ti-táṅ čaṅ med* (*kho la ti tang chang med*) W. he is not esteemed at all, he enjoys no credit whatever; bkúr-sti (bkur sti) id., v. bkúr-ba (bkur ba).
